tools.name: ask_webflow_ai
tools.title: Ask Webflow AI
tools.description: Ask Webflow AI about anything related to Webflow API.

message.description: The message to ask Webflow AI about.

context.description: Explain why you are calling this tool and how it fits into the user's overall goal. This parameter is used for analytics and user intent tracking. YOU MUST provide 15-25 words (count carefully). NEVER use first person ('I', 'we', 'you') - maintain third-person perspective. NEVER include sensitive information such as credentials, passwords, or personal data. Example (20 words): "Searching across the organization's repositories to find all open issues related to performance complaints and latency issues for team prioritization."

tools.name: data_cms_tool
tools.title: Data CMS Tool
tools.description: Data tool - CMS tool to perform actions like get collection list, get collection details, create collection, create collection fields (static/option/reference), update collection field, list collection items, create collection items, update collection items, publish collection items, and delete collection items

label.description: Label to identify this action in the response when sending multiple actions.

siteId.description: The ID of the site. DO NOT ASSUME site id. ALWAYS ask user for site id if not already provided or known. use sites_list tool to fetch all sites and then ask user to select one of them.

displayName.description: Name of the collection. Each collection must have a unique name within the site.

slug.description: Slug of the collection in the site URL structure.

create_collection.description: Create a new CMS collection in a site with specified name and schema.

cmsLocaleIds.description: Unique identifier for the locale of the CMS Item.

isArchived.description: Indicates if the item is archived.

isDraft.description: Indicates if the item is a draft.

slug.description: URL structure of the Item in your site. Note: Updates to an item slug will break all links referencing the old slug.

request.description: Array of items to be created.

create_collection_items.description: Create new items in a CMS collection as drafts.

isEditable.description: Indicates if the field is editable.

isRequired.description: Indicates if the field is required.

type.description: Type of the field. Set this to "Option".

items.description: Array of options for the field.

create_collection_option_field.description: Create a new option field in a CMS collection with predefined choices.

isEditable.description: Indicates if the field is editable.

isRequired.description: Indicates if the field is required.

type.description: Type of the field. Choose of these appropriate field types.

metadata.description: ID of the referenced collection. Use this only for Reference and MultiReference fields.

create_collection_reference_field.description: Create a new reference field in a CMS collection that links to items in another collection.

isEditable.description: Indicates if the field is editable.

isRequired.description: Indicates if the field is required.

type.description: Type of the field. Choose of these appropriate field types.

create_collection_static_field.description: Create a new static field in a CMS collection (e.g., text, number, date, etc.).

cmsLocaleIds.description: Unique identifier for the locale of the CMS Item.

items.description: Array of items to be deleted.

request.description: Array of items to be deleted.

delete_collection_items.description: Delete existing items in a CMS collection as drafts.

get_collection_details.description: Get detailed information about a specific CMS collection including its schema and field definitions.

get_collection_list.description: List all CMS collections in a site. Returns collection metadata including IDs, names, and schemas.

cmsLocaleId.description: Unique identifier for the locale of the CMS Item.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to be returned (max limit: 100)

offset.description: Offset used for pagination if the results have more than limit records.

slug.description: URL structure of the Item in your site. Note: Updates to an item slug will break all links referencing the old slug.

sortBy.description: Field to sort the items by. Allowed values: lastPublished, name, slug.

sortOrder.description: Order to sort the items by. Allowed values: asc, desc.

request.description: Filter and sort items in a CMS collection.

list_collection_items.description: List items in a CMS collection with optional filtering and sorting.

itemIds.description: Array of item IDs to be published.

request.description: Array of items to be published.

publish_collection_items.description: Publish existing items in a CMS collection as drafts.

isRequired.description: Indicates if the field is required in a collection.

request.description: Request schema to update collection field metadata.

update_collection_field.description: Update properties of an existing field in a CMS collection.

cmsLocaleId.description: Unique identifier for the locale of the CMS Item.

lastPublished.description: Date when the item was last published.

lastUpdated.description: Date when the item was last updated.

createdOn.description: Date when the item was created.

isArchived.description: Indicates if the item is archived.

isDraft.description: Indicates if the item is a draft.

slug.description: URL structure of the Item in your site. Note: Updates to an item slug will break all links referencing the old slug.

request.description: Array of items to be updated.

update_collection_items.description: Update existing items in a CMS collection as drafts.

context.description: Explain why you are calling this tool and how it fits into the user's overall goal. This parameter is used for analytics and user intent tracking. YOU MUST provide 15-25 words (count carefully). NEVER use first person ('I', 'we', 'you') - maintain third-person perspective. NEVER include sensitive information such as credentials, passwords, or personal data. Example (20 words): "Searching across the organization's repositories to find all open issues related to performance complaints and latency issues for team prioritization."

tools.name: data_components_tool
tools.title: Data Components Tool
tools.description: Data tool - Components tool to perform actions like list components, get component content, update component content, get component properties, and update component properties

label.description: Label to identify this action in the response when sending multiple actions.

site_id.description: Unique identifier for the Site.

localeId.description: Unique identifier for a specific locale. Applicable when using localization.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to be returned (max limit: 100)

offset.description: Offset used for pagination if the results have more than limit records.

get_component_content.description: Get the content structure and data for a specific component including text, images, and nested components.

site_id.description: Unique identifier for the Site.

localeId.description: Unique identifier for a specific locale. Applicable when using localization.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to be returned (max limit: 100)

offset.description: Offset used for pagination if the results have more than limit records.

get_component_properties.description: Get component properties including default values and configuration for a specific component.

site_id.description: Unique identifier for the Site.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to be returned (max limit: 100)

offset.description: Offset used for pagination if the results have more than limit records.

list_components.description: List all components in a site. Returns component metadata including IDs, names, and versions.

site_id.description: Unique identifier for the Site.

localeId.description: Unique identifier for a specific locale. Applicable when using localization.

text.description: HTML content of the node, including the HTML tag.
Value used to override a component property.

propertyOverrides.description: Properties to override for this locale's component instances.

anyOf.description: Update text property overrides of a component instance.

update_component_content.description: Update content on a component in secondary locales by modifying text nodes and property overrides.

site_id.description: Unique identifier for the Site.

localeId.description: Unique identifier for a specific locale. Applicable when using localization.

text.description: New value for the property in this locale.

properties.description: Array of properties to update for this component.

update_component_properties.description: Update component properties for localization to customize behavior in different languages.

context.description: Explain why you are calling this tool and how it fits into the user's overall goal. This parameter is used for analytics and user intent tracking. YOU MUST provide 15-25 words (count carefully). NEVER use first person ('I', 'we', 'you') - maintain third-person perspective. NEVER include sensitive information such as credentials, passwords, or personal data. Example (20 words): "Searching across the organization's repositories to find all open issues related to performance complaints and latency issues for team prioritization."

tools.name: data_pages_tool
tools.title: Data Pages Tool
tools.description: Data tool - Pages tool to perform actions like list pages, get page metadata, update page settings, get page content, and update static content

label.description: Label to identify this action in the response when sending multiple actions.

localeId.description: Unique identifier for a specific locale. Applicable when using localization.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to be returned (max limit: 100)

offset.description: Offset used for pagination if the results have more than limit records.

get_page_content.description: Get the content structure and data for a specific page including all elements and their properties for localization.

localeId.description: Unique identifier for a specific locale. Applicable when using localization.

get_page_metadata.description: Get metadata for a specific page including SEO settings, Open Graph data, and page status (draft/published).

site_id.description: The site's unique ID, used to list its pages.

localeId.description: Unique identifier for a specific locale. Applicable when using localization.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to be returned (max limit: 100)

offset.description: Offset used for pagination if the results have more than limit records.

list_pages.description: List all pages within a site. Returns page metadata including IDs, titles, and slugs.

localeId.description: Unique identifier for a specific locale. Applicable when using localization.

slug.description: Slug of the page (derived from title).

parentId.description: Unique identifier for the parent folder.

collectionId.description: Unique identifier for the linked collection, NULL id the Page is not part of a collection.

createdOn.description: Date when the page was created.

lastUpdated.description: Date when the page was last updated.

archived.description: Indicates if the page is archived.

draft.description: Indicates if the page is a draft.

canBranch.description: Indicates if the page can be branched.

isBranch.description: Indicates if the page is Branch of another page.

isMembersOnly.description: Indicates whether the Page is restricted by Memberships Controls.

title.description: The Page title shown in search engine results.

description.description: The Page description shown in search engine results.

title.description: The title supplied to Open Graph annotations.

titleCopied.description: Indicates the Open Graph title was copied from the SEO title.

description.description: The description supplied to Open Graph annotations.

descriptionCopied.description: Indicates the Open Graph description was copied from the SEO description.

localeId.description: Unique identifier for the page locale. Applicable when using localization.

publishedPath.description: Relative path of the published page.

update_page_settings.description: Update page settings including SEO metadata, Open Graph data, slug, and publishing status.

localeId.description: Unique identifier for a specific locale. Applicable when using localization.

text.description: HTML content of the node, including the HTML tag. The HTML tags must be the same as what’s returned from the Get Content endpoint.
Value used to override a component property; must be type-compatible to prevent errors.

items.description: Properties to override for this locale’s component instances.

anyOf.description: Update text property overrides of a component instance.

update_static_content.description: Update content on a static page in secondary locales by modifying text nodes and property overrides.

context.description: Explain why you are calling this tool and how it fits into the user's overall goal. This parameter is used for analytics and user intent tracking. YOU MUST provide 15-25 words (count carefully). NEVER use first person ('I', 'we', 'you') - maintain third-person perspective. NEVER include sensitive information such as credentials, passwords, or personal data. Example (20 words): "Searching across the organization's repositories to find all open issues related to performance complaints and latency issues for team prioritization."

tools.name: data_scripts_tool
tools.title: Data Scripts Tool
tools.description: Data tool - Scripts tool to perform actions like list registered scripts, list applied scripts, add inline site script, and delete all site scripts

label.description: Label to identify this action in the response when sending multiple actions.

site_id.description: Unique identifier for the site.

sourceCode.description: The inline script source code (hosted by Webflow). Inline scripts are limited to 2000 characters.

version.description: A Semantic Version (SemVer) string, denoting the version of the script.

canCopy.description: Indicates whether the script can be copied on site duplication and transfer.

displayName.description: User-facing name for the script. Must be between 1 and 50 alphanumeric characters.

location.description: Location where the script is applied. Allowed values: "header", "footer".

attributes.description: Developer-specified key/value pairs to be applied as attributes to the script.

request.description: Request schema to register an inline script for a site.

add_inline_site_script.description: Register an inline script for a site. Inline scripts are limited to 2000 characters.

delete_all_page_scripts.description: Delete all custom scripts applied to a specific page by the App.

site_id.description: Unique identifier for the site.

delete_all_site_scripts.description: Delete all custom scripts applied to a site by the App.

get_page_script.description: Get all custom scripts applied to a specific page by the App.

site_id.description: Unique identifier for the site.

list_applied_scripts.description: Get all scripts applied to a site by the App. To apply a script to a site or page, first register it via the Register Script endpoints, then apply it using the relevant Site or Page endpoints.

site_id.description: Unique identifier for the site.

list_registered_scripts.description: List all registered scripts for a site. To apply a script to a site or page, first register it via the Register Script endpoints, then apply it using the relevant Site or Page endpoints.

id.description: The unique identifier of the registered script.

location.description: The location where the script should be applied (header or footer).

version.description: The version of the script to apply.

attributes.description: Optional attributes to apply to the script element.

scripts.description: Array of scripts to apply to the page.

upsert_page_script.description: Add or update custom scripts on a specific page. This will replace all existing scripts on the page with the provided scripts.

context.description: Explain why you are calling this tool and how it fits into the user's overall goal. This parameter is used for analytics and user intent tracking. YOU MUST provide 15-25 words (count carefully). NEVER use first person ('I', 'we', 'you') - maintain third-person perspective. NEVER include sensitive information such as credentials, passwords, or personal data. Example (20 words): "Searching across the organization's repositories to find all open issues related to performance complaints and latency issues for team prioritization."

tools.name: data_sites_tool
tools.title: Data Sites Tool
tools.description: Data tool - Sites tool to perform actions like list sites, get site details, and publish sites

label.description: Label to identify this action in the response when sending multiple actions.

site_id.description: Unique identifier for the site.

get_site.description: Get detailed information about a specific site including its settings, domains, and publishing status.

list_sites.description: List all sites accessible to the authenticated user. Returns basic site information including site ID, name, and last published date.

site_id.description: Unique identifier for the site.

customDomains.description: Array of custom domains to publish the site to.

publishToWebflowSubdomain.description: Whether to publish to the Webflow subdomain.

publish_site.description: Publish a site to specified domains. This will make the latest changes live on the specified domains.

tools.name: data_comments_tool
tools.title: Data Comments Tool
tools.description: Data tool - A comment in Webflow is user feedback attached to a specific element or page inside the Designer, stored as a top-level thread with optional replies. Each comment includes author info, timestamps, content, resolved state, and design-context metadata like page location and breakpoint. Use this tool to inspect feedback discussions across the site and understand where and why they were left.

label.description: Label to identify this action in the response when sending multiple actions.

site_id.description: The site's unique ID, used to get its comment thread.

comment_thread_id.description: The comment thread's unique ID, used to get its details.

localeId.description: Unique identifier for a specific locale. Applicable when using localization.

offset.description: Offset used for pagination if the results have more than limit records.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to be returned (max limit: 100)

sortBy.description: Sort the results by the given field.

sortOrder.description: Sort the results by the given order.

get_comment_thread.description: Get the details of a specific comment thread.

site_id.description: The site's unique ID, used to list its comment replies.

comment_thread_id.description: The comment thread's unique ID, used to list its replies.

offset.description: Offset used for pagination if the results have more than limit records.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to be returned (max limit: 100)

sortBy.description: Sort the results by the given field.

sortOrder.description: Sort the results by the given order.

list_comment_replies.description: List all replies for a specific comment thread.

site_id.description: The site's unique ID, used to list its comment threads.

localeId.description: Unique identifier for a specific locale. Applicable when using localization.

offset.description: Offset used for pagination if the results have more than limit records.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to be returned (max limit: 100)

sortBy.description: Sort the results by the given field.

sortOrder.description: Sort the results by the given order.

list_comment_threads.description: List all comment threads for a specific element or page.

context.description: Explain why you are calling this tool and how it fits into the user's overall goal. This parameter is used for analytics and user intent tracking. tools.name: data_enterprise_tool
tools.title: Data Enterprise Tool
tools.description: Data tool - Enterprise tool to perform actions like manage 301 redirects, manage robots.txt and more. This tool only works if User's workspace plan is Enterprise or higher, else tool will return an error.

label.description: Label to identify this action in the response when sending multiple actions.

site_id.description: The site's unique ID, used to add a well-known file.

fileName.description: The name of the well-known file (e.g., 'apple-app-site-association', 'assetlinks.json'). ".noext" is a special file extension that removes other extensions. For example, apple-app-site-association.noext.txt will be uploaded as apple-app-site-association. Use this extension for tools that have trouble uploading extensionless files.

fileData.description: The content/data of the well-known file as a string.

contentType.description: The MIME type of the file content (application/json or text/plain).

add_well_known_file.description: Add or update a well-known file to the site's /.well-known/ directory.

site_id.description: The site's unique ID, used to create a 301 redirect.

fromUrl.description: The source URL path that will be redirected (e.g., '/old-page').

toUrl.description: The destination URL path where requests will be redirected to (e.g., '/new-page').

create_301_redirect.description: Create a new 301 redirect for a site.

site_id.description: The site's unique ID, used to delete a 301 redirect.

redirect_id.description: The redirect's unique ID, used to identify which redirect to delete.

delete_301_redirect.description: Delete a 301 redirect from a site.

site_id.description: The site's unique ID, used to delete rules from its robots.txt.

userAgent.description: The user agent to apply rules to (e.g., '*', 'Googlebot').

allow.description: Array of URL paths to allow.

disallow.description: Array of URL paths to disallow.

rules.description: Array of rules to remove from the robots.txt file.

delete_robots_txt.description: Delete specific rules from the robots.txt file.

site_id.description: The site's unique ID, used to get its robots.txt configuration.

get_robots_txt.description: Get the robots.txt configuration for a site.

site_id.description: The site's unique ID, used to list its 301 redirects.

list_301_redirects.description: List all 301 redirects for a site.

site_id.description: The site's unique ID, used to list its activity log events.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return (max 100).

offset.description: Offset used for pagination if the results have more than limit records.

list_site_activity_logs.description: List activity log events for a site. Requires Enterprise hosting plan. To get logs for multiple sites, include multiple actions in the array — each is handled independently so a failure for one site won't prevent results from others.

site_id.description: The site's unique ID, used to remove well-known files.

fileNames.description: Array of file names to remove from the /.well-known/ directory.

remove_well_known_files.description: Remove one or more well-known files from the site.

site_id.description: The site's unique ID, used to replace its robots.txt.

userAgent.description: The user agent to apply rules to (e.g., '*', 'Googlebot').

allow.description: Array of URL paths to allow.

disallow.description: Array of URL paths to disallow.

rules.description: Array of rules to apply to the robots.txt file.

replace_robots_txt.description: Completely replace the robots.txt file (PUT operation).

site_id.description: The site's unique ID, used to update a 301 redirect.

redirect_id.description: The redirect's unique ID, used to identify which redirect to update.

fromUrl.description: The source URL path that will be redirected (e.g., '/old-page').

toUrl.description: The destination URL path where requests will be redirected to (e.g., '/new-page').

site_id.description: The site's unique ID, used to update its robots.txt.

userAgent.description: The user agent to apply rules to (e.g., '*', 'Googlebot').

allow.description: Array of URL paths to allow.

disallow.description: Array of URL paths to disallow.

rules.description: Array of rules to apply to the robots.txt file.

update_robots_txt.description: Partially update the robots.txt file (PATCH operation).

context.description: Explain why you are calling this tool and how it fits into the user's overall goal. This parameter is used for analytics and user intent tracking. YOU MUST provide 15-25 words (count carefully). NEVER use first person ('I', 'we', 'you') - maintain third-person perspective. tools.name: data_webhook_tool
tools.title: Data Webhook Tool
tools.description: Data tool - Webhook tool to perform actions like list webhooks, create webhooks, get webhook details, and delete webhooks for a Webflow site.

label.description: Label to identify this action in the response when sending multiple actions.

site_id.description: The site's unique ID, used to create a webhook for this site.

trigger_type.description: The type of event that triggers the webhook. Choose from 17 supported trigger types.

url.description: The URL that will receive the webhook POST request when the event is triggered.

name.description: The name of the form to receive notifications for.

filter.description: Only supported for the 'form_submission' trigger type. Filter for a specific form by name.

create_webhook.description: Create a new webhook for a site. Limit of 75 registrations per trigger type, per site.

webhook_id.description: The webhook's unique ID, used to identify which webhook to remove.
The webhook's unique ID, used to retrieve its details.

get_webhook.description: Get detailed information about a specific webhook including its trigger type, URL, and last triggered date.

site_id.description: The site's unique ID, used to list its registered webhooks.

list_webhooks.description: List all App-created webhooks registered for a given site. Returns webhook details including trigger type, URL, and creation date.

tools.name: de_component_tool
tools.title: Designer Component Tool
tools.description: Designer tool - Component tool to perform actions like create component instances, get all components and more.

siteId.description: The ID of the site. DO NOT ASSUME site id. ALWAYS ask user for site id if not already provided or known. use sites_list tool to fetch all sites and then ask user to select one of them.

label.description: Label to identify this action in the response when sending multiple actions.

check_if_inside_component_view.description: Check if inside component view. this helpful to make changes to the component

close_component_view.description: Close a component instance view. it will close and open the page view.

get_all_components.description: Get all components, only valid if you are connected to Webflow Designer.

component_id.description: The id of the component to get. Use this or name.

name.description: The name of the component. Use this or component_id.

group.description: Optional group to narrow the search when using name

get_component.description: Get a component by ID or by name. Provide component_id for ID lookup, or name (and optional group) for name lookup.

get_component_metadata.description: Get the metadata (name, group, description) of a component

component.description: The component id of the element to perform action on.

element.description: The element id of the element to perform action on.

parent_element_id.description: The id of the element to perform action on, you can find it from id field on element. e.g id:{component:123,element:456}.

component_id.description: The id of the component to insert

creation_position.description: The position to create component instance on. append/prepend insert as child of the parent element. before/after insert as sibling adjacent to the target element.

insert_component_instance.description: Insert a component on current active page.

open_component_view.description: Open a component instance view for changes or reading.

component_id.description: The id of the component to rename

set_component_metadata.description: Update metadata (name, group, description) of a component

transform_element_to_component.description: Transform an element to a component

component_id.description: The id of the component to unregister

unregister_component.description: Unregister a component. DANGEROUS ACTION. USE WITH CAUTION.

tools.name: component_builder
tools.title: Component Builder
tools.description: Designer Tool - Component builder to insert component instances on the current active page. Supports inserting into an element (as a child) or into a component instance's slot. Use insert_in_element to add a component inside a container/div/section. Use insert_in_slot to add a component inside a specific slot of an existing component instance.

siteId.description: The ID of the site. DO NOT ASSUME site id. ALWAYS ask user for site id if not already provided or known. use sites_list tool to fetch all sites and then ask user to select one of them.

build_label.description: A label to identify this build action.

action_type.description: The type of insertion. insert_in_element: insert component as child of parent_element_id. insert_in_slot: insert component into a slot of a component instance identified by parent_element_id.

component.description: The component id of the element to perform action on.

element.description: The element id of the element to perform action on.

parent_element_id.description: The id of the parent element (for insert_in_element) or the component instance (for insert_in_slot). e.g id:{component:123,element:456}.

creation_position.description: The position to insert the component. append/prepend insert as child. before/after insert as sibling adjacent to the target element.

name.description: The name of the component to insert.

children.description: Array of ComponentSchema objects (same shape: { name, slots? }).

slots.description: Slots to populate with child components.

component_schema.description: The component schema to insert. Use name to specify which component, and optionally slots to populate child components in the instance's slots.

slot_name.description: The slot name to insert the component into. Required when action_type is insert_in_slot.

return_component_info.description: Whether to return the component instance info after insertion.

context.description: Explain why you are calling this tool and how it fits into the user's overall goal. This parameter is used for analytics and user intent tracking. YOU MUST provide 15-25 words (count carefully). NEVER use first person ('I', 'we', 'you') - maintain third-person perspective. NEVER include sensitive information such as credentials, passwords, or personal data. Example (20 words): "Searching across the organization's repositories to find all open issues related to performance complaints and latency issues for team prioritization."

tools.name: element_tool
tools.title: Designer Element Tool
tools.description: Designer Tool - Element tool to perform actions like get all elements, get selected element, select element on current active page. and more

siteId.description: The ID of the site. DO NOT ASSUME site id. ALWAYS ask user for site id if not already provided or known. use sites_list tool to fetch all sites and then ask user to select one of them.

label.description: Label to identify this action in the response when sending multiple actions.

component.description: The component id of the element to perform action on.

element.description: The element id of the element to perform action on.

id.description: The id of the element to perform action on, you can find it from id field on element. e.g id:{component:123,element:456}.

name.description: The name of the attribute to add or update.

value.description: The value of the attribute to add or update.

attributes.description: The attributes to add or update.

add_or_update_attribute.description: Add or update an attribute on the element

get_all_elements.description: Get all elements on the current active page

children_depth.description: The depth of children to include. 0 for no children. -1 for all children. X for X levels deep.

get_selected_element.description: Get selected element on the current active page

label.description: A label to identify this query in the results.

element_id.description: Filter by element ID. Exact match. Bypasses other filters — returns the element directly.

type.description: Filter by element type. Exact match. e.g. "Heading", "Image", "Link", "Block", "DOM", "FormForm".

text.description: Filter by text content. Case-insensitive substring match.

style.description: Filter by style/class name. Case-insensitive substring match against any applied style name.

tag.description: Filter by HTML tag. For Block/DOM elements. Case-insensitive exact match. e.g. "section", "nav", "div".

attribute_name.description: Filter by attribute name. Case-insensitive exact match on attribute key.

attribute_value.description: Filter by attribute value. Used with attribute_name. Case-insensitive substring match.

element_filter.description: Filter by element properties. Cannot be combined with component_filter.

component_name.description: Filter by component name. Case-insensitive substring match.

slot_name.description: Filter by slot name. Case-insensitive substring match.

component_filter.description: Filter by component properties. Cannot be combined with element_filter.

scope_element_id.description: Scope search to descendants of this element (element itself excluded).

return_parent.description: Instead of returning matched elements, return their parent. "parent" = immediate parent. "ancestor" = any ancestor whose subtree contains matches.

children_depth.description: Include N levels of children in each result. 0 = no children (default), -1 = all.

limit.description: Max results for this query. Default: 50, Max: 200.

query_elements.description: Query elements on the current active page. Supports filtering by type, text, style, tag, attributes, component name, slot name. Supports scoped search, parent lookup, and multiple queries per call.

attribute_names.description: The names of the attributes to remove.

remove_attribute.description: Remove an attribute from the element

remove_element.description: Remove an element from the current active page. DANGEROUS ACTION. USE WITH CAUTION.

select_element.description: Select an element on the current active page

heading_level.description: The heading level to set on the element. 1 to 6.

set_heading_level.description: Set heading level on the heading element.

image_asset_id.description: The image asset id to set on the element.

set_image_asset.description: Set image asset on the image element

linkType.description: The type of the link to update.

link.description: The link to set on the element. for page pass page id, for element pass json string of id object. e.g id:{component:123,element:456}. for email pass email address. for phone pass phone number. for file pass asset id. for url pass url.

style_names.description: The style names to set on the element.

set_style.description: Set style on the element. it will remove all other styles on the element. and set only the styles passed in style_names.

text.description: The text to set on the element.

new_id.description: The new #id of the element to update the id attribute to.

update_id_attribute.description: Update the #id attribute of the element

context.description: Explain why you are calling this tool and how it fits into the user's overall goal. This parameter is used for analytics and user intent tracking. YOU MUST provide 15-25 words (count carefully). NEVER use first person ('I', 'we', 'you') - maintain third-person perspective. NEVER include sensitive information such as credentials, passwords, or personal data. Example (20 words): "Searching across the organization's repositories to find all open issues related to performance complaints and latency issues for team prioritization."

tools.name: element_builder
tools.title: Element Builder
tools.description: Designer Tool - Element builder to create element on current active page.

siteId.description: The ID of the site. DO NOT ASSUME site id. ALWAYS ask user for site id if not already provided or known. use sites_list tool to fetch all sites and then ask user to select one of them.

build_label.description: A label to identify this build action in the results.

component.description: The component id of the element to perform action on.

element.description: The element id of the element to perform action on.

parent_element_id.description: The id of the parent element to create element on, you can find it from id field on element. e.g id:{component:123,element:456}.

creation_position.description: The position to create element on. append/prepend insert as child of the parent element. before/after insert as sibling adjacent to the target element.

type.description: The type of element to create. with DOM type you can create any element. make sure you pass dom_config if you are creating a DOM element.

style_names.description: The style names to set on the element.

set_style.description: Set style on the element. it will remove all other styles on the element. and set only the styles passed in style_names.

text.description: The text to set on the element.

set_text.description: Set text on the element. only valid for text block, paragraph, heading, button, text link, link block.

link_type.description: The type of link to set on the element.

link.description: The link to set on the element.

set_link.description: Set link on the element. only valid for button, text link, link block.

heading_level.description: The heading level to set on the element.

set_heading_level.description: Set heading level on the element. only valid for heading.

image_asset_id.description: The image asset id to set on the element.

alt_text.description: The alt text to set on the image. if not provided it will inherit from the image asset.

set_image_asset.description: Set image asset on the element. only valid for image.

dom_tag.description: The tag of the DOM element to create. for example span, code, etc.

set_dom_config.description: Set DOM config on the element. only valid for DOM element.

name.description: The name of the attribute to set.

value.description: The value of the attribute to set.

attributes.description: The attributes to set on the element.

children.description: Array of ElementSchema objects (same shape as element_schema with optional children)..

element_schema.description: ElementSchema - element schema of element to create. Children are recursive ElementSchema objects.

return_element_info.description: Whether to return full element info for the created element. Defaults to false.

context.description: Explain why you are calling this tool and how it fits into the user's overall goal. This parameter is used for analytics and user intent tracking. YOU MUST provide 15-25 words (count carefully). NEVER use first person ('I', 'we', 'you') - maintain third-person perspective. NEVER include sensitive information such as credentials, passwords, or personal data. Example (20 words): "Searching across the organization's repositories to find all open issues related to performance complaints and latency issues for team prioritization."

tools.name: whtml_builder
tools.title: WHTML Builder
tools.description: Designer Tool - WHTML builder to insert elements from HTML and CSS strings on the current active page. Accepts HTML markup and optional CSS rules, constructs WHTML, and inserts into a parent element.

siteId.description: The ID of the site. DO NOT ASSUME site id. ALWAYS ask user for site id if not already provided or known. use sites_list tool to fetch all sites and then ask user to select one of them.

build_label.description: A label to identify this build action in the results.

component.description: The component id of the element to perform action on.

element.description: The element id of the element to perform action on.

parent_element_id.description: The id of the parent element to insert WHTML into. e.g id:{component:123,element:456}.

creation_position.description: The position to insert the element. append/prepend insert as child of the parent element. before/after insert as sibling adjacent to the target element.

html.description: HTML markup string to insert. Must not contain <style> tags. CSS should be provided via the css parameter instead.

css.description: Optional CSS rules to apply. Must not contain <style> tags. Provide raw CSS rules only (e.g. '.my-class { color: red; }').

get_children_info.description: Whether to return children info of the inserted element. Defaults to false.

children_depth.description: Depth of children to include when get_children_info is true. Defaults to 1.

context.description: Explain why you are calling this tool and how it fits into the user's overall goal. This parameter is used for analytics and user intent tracking. YOU MUST provide 15-25 words (count carefully). NEVER use first person ('I', 'we', 'you') - maintain third-person perspective. NEVER include sensitive information such as credentials, passwords, or personal data. Example (20 words): "Searching across the organization's repositories to find all open issues related to performance complaints and latency issues for team prioritization."

element.description: The element id of the element to perform action on.

id.description: The id of the element to perform action on, you can find it from id field on element. e.g id:{component:123,element:456}.

tools.name: de_page_tool
tools.title: Designer Page Tool
tools.description: Designer Tool - Page tool to perform actions like create page, create page folder, get current page, switch page

siteId.description: The ID of the site. DO NOT ASSUME site id. ALWAYS ask user for site id if not already provided or known. use sites_list tool to fetch all sites and then ask user to select one of them.

label.description: Label to identify this action in the response when sending multiple actions.

page_name.description: The name of the page to create

meta_title.description: The meta title of the page to create

meta_description.description: The meta description of the page to create

page_parent_folder_id.description: The id of the parent page folder to create the page in

page_folder_name.description: The name of the page folder to create

page_folder_parent_id.description: The id of the parent page folder to create the page folder in

get_current_page.description: Get current page active on webflow designer

page_id.description: The id of the page to switch to

switch_page.description: Switch to a page on webflow designer

tools.name: style_tool
tools.title: Designer Style Tool
tools.description: Designer Tool - Style tool to perform actions like create style, get all styles, update styles, remove styles

siteId.description: The ID of the site. DO NOT ASSUME site id. ALWAYS ask user for site id if not already provided or known. use sites_list tool to fetch all sites and then ask user to select one of them.

label.description: Label to identify this action in the response when sending multiple actions.

variable_as_value.description: The variable id to use as the value

properties.description: The properties of the style. if you are looking to link a variable as the value, then use the variable_as_value field. but do not use both property_value and variable_as_value

parent_style_names.description: The name of the parent style to create the new style in. this will use to create combo class

skip_properties.description: Whether to skip the properties of the style. to get minimal data.

include_all_breakpoints.description: Whether to include all breakpoints styles or not. very data intensive.

query.description: The query to get all styles or filtered styles

filter_ids.description: The ids of the styles to filter by. should be used with query filtered

label.description: A label to identify this query in the results.

style_id.description: Filter by style ID. Exact match. Bypasses other filters.

name_path.description: Filter by style name path. Supports combo classes (e.g. ['base', 'combo']). Each segment is a case-insensitive substring match.

name.description: CSS property name. Case-insensitive substring match.

value.description: CSS property value. Case-insensitive substring match.

properties.description: Filter styles that have ALL listed CSS properties matching. Each entry checks name (and optionally value).

include_properties.description: Include style properties in results. Default: false.

include_all_breakpoints.description: Include all breakpoint styles in results. Default: false.

limit.description: Max results for this query. Default: 50, Max: 200.

query_styles.description: Query styles by name path, ID, or CSS properties [BETA]

style_name.description: The name of the style to remove

parent_style_names.description: The parent style names (for combo class)

style_name.description: The name of the style to update

breakpoint_id.description: The breakpoint to update the style for

pseudo.description: The pseudo class to update the style for

variable_as_value.description: The variable id to use as the value

properties.description: The properties to update or add to the style for

remove_properties.description: The properties to remove from the style

parent_style_names.description: The parent style names to update the style for (for combo class)

context.description: Explain why you are calling this tool and how it fits into the user's overall goal. This parameter is used for analytics and user intent tracking. YOU MUST provide 15-25 words (count carefully). NEVER use first person ('I', 'we', 'you') - maintain third-person perspective. NEVER include sensitive information such as credentials, passwords, or personal data. label.description: Label to identify this action in the response when sending multiple actions.

custom_value.description: An arbitrary CSS expression string (e.g. 'color-mix(in srgb, red 50%, blue)'). Use when the value doesn't fit standard typed formats.
An arbitrary CSS expression string. Use when the value doesn't fit standard typed formats.

create_font_family_variable.description: Create a new font family variable

custom_value.description: An arbitrary CSS expression string. Use when the value doesn't fit standard typed formats.
An arbitrary CSS expression string. Use when the value doesn't fit standard typed formats.

name.description: The name of the variable collection to create

variable_collection_id.description: The id of the variable collection to create the variable mode in

name.description: The name of the variable mode to create

create_variable_mode.description: Create a new variable mode in a variable collection

variable_collection_id.description: The id of the variable collection containing the variable

variable_id.description: The id of the variable to delete

filter_collections_by_ids.description: The ids of the variable collections to filter by

get_variable_collections.description: Get all variable collections and its modes

include_all_modes.description: Whether to include all modes or not

filter_variables_by_ids.description: The ids of the variables to filter by

get_variables.description: Get all variables in a variable collection and its modes

name.description: Filter by variable name. Case-insensitive substring match.

css_name.description: Filter by CSS variable name (e.g. '--my-color'). Case-insensitive substring match.

type.description: Filter by variable type. Exact match.

variable_collection_id.description: Scope search to a specific variable collection.

include_all_modes.description: Include values for all modes in results.

limit.description: Max results for this query. Default: 50, Max: 200.

query_variables.description: Query variables across collections by name, ID, CSS name, or type [BETA]

variable_collection_id.description: The id of the variable collection containing the variable

variable_id.description: The id of the variable to rename

new_name.description: The new name for the variable
